question.text: FPIs are allowed to participate in Indian Exchange Traded Commodity Derivatives (ETCDs) in which type of contracts?

answer.text: Cash-settled non-agricultural commodity derivative contracts and indices
All commodity derivative contracts including agricultural
Only gold and silver futures contracts

explanation.text: **The Logic:** FPIs are allowed to participate in cash-settled non-agricultural commodity derivative contracts and indices comprising such non-agricultural commodities, as amended by SEBI in 2022. **The Trap:** FPIs cannot participate in agricultural commodity derivatives or physically-settled contracts. The restriction is not limited to gold/silver alone. **Cross-Exam Context:** Series VIII covers FPI participation in equity derivatives. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** This restriction protects sensitive agricultural commodity markets from speculative foreign flows while allowing FPIs to hedge non-agri commodity exposures.
